entries.4ee5ac6c4c7f: Aryan's Quest — The Road to Rebuild Nalanda
entries.a722be1ae689: An educational historical RPG set in 1203 AD. Travel across six Asian civilizations to rebuild the greatest center of learning in the ancient world.
entries.d556273a77c4: Aryan, at the Gates of Nalanda
entries.760b10cfadfb: 1193 AD. Foreign invaders sweep through northern India.
entries.d0fac25a7f58: Nalanda — the greatest university the world had ever seen — is set on fire.
entries.1373fc57adda: Nine million manuscripts. Ten thousand students. Two thousand teachers.
entries.e898dc05470a: The fire burns for three months.
entries.7165e40a616d: Every book, every scroll, every word of human knowledge — reduced to ash.
entries.a358d1ff868f: The world does not yet know what it has lost.
entries.054c9957e3a8: My father described Nalanda like a second sky — so vast, so full of light.
entries.4ad7efc01973: He said walking its corridors felt like touching everything humanity had ever known.
entries.d5f49d7f54a0: Then the news reached our village. Foreign invaders. Fire. No survivors.
entries.f37a7e873bfd: My father was one of the scholars who could not escape.
entries.00a3913de053: I left home the same day the news arrived.
entries.34fc81efdcb2: Months later, I arrived at the gates. Nothing remained but ash and silence.
entries.4328da2b8ec4: Nine million manuscripts. Gone. Every word my father ever read — gone.
entries.1f74838fd057: But knowledge does not vanish. It moves. It hides. It waits.
entries.ae9804cb993b: Then it's my mission now — to gather what was lost, and rebuild Nalanda. For my father.
entries.00b0b023bb2a: King Balaputradeva funded a monastery at Nalanda. Srivijayan monks studied there for generations.
entries.5ce38be9bae2: Japanese monks revered Nalanda almost as a holy place. Kukai and Ennin traced their knowledge back to Nalanda's teachings.
entries.0300e29fe177: The Tripitaka Koreana — 81,000 wooden blocks containing the entire Buddhist canon — was being carved in this era. Much of it traces to Nalanda.
entries.193b91c33274: Chinese monk Xuanzang spent 17 years at Nalanda and brought back 657 texts. The Chinese revered Nalanda mythologically.
entries.003385ba56db: Tibetan monks who studied at Nalanda carried thousands of manuscripts back before the fire. Tibet preserved Nalanda's own soul.
entries.7e75b07fa037: Answer Dharmakirti's question to unlock your trial.
entries.e1a6cc3b0572: Tiles glow all at once — select them all from memory
entries.e7c0dc184240: The tiles remember what the fire tried to erase.
entries.a1eabe7e0cca: Select a manuscript to read its excerpt
entries.b19f29690a45: Answer {expression0}''s question to unlock your trial.
entries.ed268c600f0d: Connect all islands. Each number shows how many bridges leave that island.
entries.289916b80919: Click an island, then click another to place a bridge. Click again to add a second bridge. Click a third time to remove.
entries.63ec6c65088d: Quest World Lingo was built as part of Hackathon '#'3 hosted by <span0>Lingo.dev</span0> — a platform that makes localizing apps beautifully simple. A huge thank you to the Lingo.dev team for the inspiration and the tools.
entries.080c21657e5f: The idea behind this game is simple: puzzle games are for everyone — kids, adults, anyone who enjoys a good mental challenge. But I wanted to go a step further and weave real history into the gameplay. Every kingdom you visit, every scholar you meet, every artifact you collect is rooted in actual historical events from 1203 AD — the year Nalanda University was destroyed.
entries.f14f5df6e710: The story follows Aryan, a young student from the ruins of Nalanda, who travels across five kingdoms of medieval Asia to recover lost knowledge. Along the way, players solve puzzles, meet historical scholars, and piece together a forgotten world.
entries.1dc6fa0dfa6a: Originally, I wanted each scholar to be a live AI — players could speak to Dharmakirti, Kukai, Monk Uicheon, Shen Kuo, and Sakya Pandita in real time, ask them anything about history, and earn wisdom through genuine conversation. Due to time constraints and a desire to keep the game accessible without added complexity, that vision had to stay on the drawing board for now. Maybe one day.
entries.d0248e688749: Five kingdoms. Five games. One purpose. Rebuild Nalanda.
entries.bc0cbc0af0d6: Most of this game is fully translated using Lingo.dev — the world map, kingdom cards, puzzle games, and scholar dialogue all adapt to your chosen language. However, some content remains in English only: the artifact excerpts and historical significance notes in the Knowledge Chest, and this About Game page. The Knowledge Chest content is stored as plain data strings rather than JSX text, which means Lingo.dev's compiler could not automatically pick them up for translation. This About Game page was also kept in English intentionally, given the large volume of text and the complexity of translating it all at once within the hackathon timeline.
entries.4f464ccb9d39: The core gameplay — world map, kingdom cards, puzzle games, and scholar dialogue — is fully available in multiple languages powered by Lingo.dev. The manuscript excerpts and historical significance notes in the Knowledge Chest, as well as this About Game page, are in English only. This was a deliberate technical choice: the game content that players interact with most is translated, while the deeper reference material and meta information were scoped to English to keep the project focused.
